name: Mikolov et al., 'Efficient Estimation of Word Representations', 2013

task.description: Create a self-contained 1920x1080 HTML slide titled 'word2vec: CBOW vs Skip-gram (Mikolov et al., 2013)'. TOP HALF: TWO equal-width side-by-side architecture panels with identical top/bottom edges and a central gutter. LEFT 'CBOW': inline-SVG with context words 'w(t-2), w(t-1), w(t+1), w(t+2)' projecting into a shared 'SUM/projection' node predicting 'w(t)'. RIGHT 'Skip-gram': single input 'w(t)' projecting out to predict the same four context words. BOTTOM HALF: a full-width row of THREE coexisting equal-height blocks sharing top and bottom edges with uniform gutters: (1) BLOCK 'Objective': the skip-gram softmax 'p(w_O | w_I) = exp(v'_{w_O}^T v_{w_I}) / sum_{w=1}^{W} exp(v'_w^T v_{w_I})' with real sub/superscripts; (2) BLOCK 'Settings': a 4-row table ('dim = 300', 'window = 5', 'negative = 5', 'epochs = 3'); (3) BLOCK 'Analogies': FOUR cards 'king - man + woman ~= queen', 'Paris - France + Italy ~= Rome', 'big - bigger + small ~= smaller', 'Tokyo - Japan + Russia ~= Moscow'. Footnote at bottom.
requirements: The two architecture panels are equal width and share the same top and bottom edge
The three bottom blocks are equal height, share the same top and bottom edge, with uniform gutters between them
In CBOW all four context inputs connect into the shared node, and in Skip-gram the single input fans out to all four, without arrow-label overlap
The softmax objective renders its subscripts (w_O, w_I) and the superscript T and the sum bounds, fully inside its block (no horizontal overflow)
All four 'Settings' rows are present and fully inside their block
All four analogy cards are present, equal width within the block, with no clipped text
No element overlaps any other element on the slide
Nothing overflows the 1920x1080 canvas; no scrollbar
The footnote is fully visible above the bottom edge and overlaps nothing
Every text element is at least 12px
task.paper: Mikolov et al., 'Efficient Estimation of Word Representations', 2013

ss_html:
word2vec: CBOW vs Skip-gram (Mikolov et al., 2013)
CBOW
Skip-gram
Objective
p(wO | wI) =

exp(v'wOT vwI)
────────────────────────
Σw=1W exp(v'wT vwI)
Settings
dim 300
window 5
negative 5
epochs 3
Analogies
king - man + woman
~= queen
Paris - France + Italy
~= Rome
big - bigger + small
~= smaller
Tokyo - Japan + Russia
~= Moscow
Mikolov, T., Chen, K., Corrado, G., & Dean, J. (2013). Efficient estimation of word representations in vector space. arXiv preprint arXiv:1301.3781.

Let me create a comprehensive HTML slide for word2vec CBOW vs Skip-gram. I'll plan the layout carefully: **Slide dimensions:** 1920×1080px **Layout:** - Header/Title: ~100px - TOP HALF: Two architecture panels (CBOW + Skip-gram) side by side - BOTTOM HALF: Three equal-width blocks (Objective, Settings, Analogies) - Footnote at bottom Let me plan the spacing: - Title area: 80px (top: 20px, height: 60px) - Top panels: from y=90 to y=530 (440px height) - Bottom blocks: from y=550 to y=990 (440px height) - Footnote: y=1000 to y=1060 (60px) Now I'm calculating the widths with 30px margins on each side and accounting for gutters between sections. The top panels each get 920px width with a 20px gap between them, while the three bottom blocks are roughly 606px wide each with 40px total spacing between them. I'm going with a dark theme using a very dark blue background with slightly lighter panels, cyan accents, and orange highlights for contrast. For the CBOW diagram, I'll show the context words on the left feeding into a central projection node with arrows, then a single arrow pointing to the target word on the right. The Skip-gram will flip this—a single input word on the left with arrows fanning out to the context words on the right. Now I'm laying out the SVG coordinates within each panel, positioning the CBOW input nodes vertically on the left, the sum node in the center, and the output on the right, while Skip-gram mirrors this with the input centered and outputs spread across the right side. Writing the HTML layout... Now I'm finalizing the exact positioning for the three bottom blocks with proper spacing and gutters, making sure the third block uses the remaining width. With the layout locked in, I'm ready to start building out the SVG visualizations for the CBOW and Skip-gram model diagrams. Writing SVG code... Writing the formula markup... I'm working through the layout proportions for the slide—need to fit the title, two content sections, gaps, and footnote into the 1080px height while keeping everything balanced. I'm finalizing the positioning so everything fits within the 1080px height—the title, top panels, bottom blocks, and footnote all stack with precise gaps between them. Now I'm ready to write out the complete HTML structure with SVG arrows using marker elements for the arrowheads. I'm locking in the design details: dark navy background with slate panels, cyan and amber accents, and specific typography. For the CBOW diagram, I'm positioning input labels and circles on the left, a central SUM node, and the output on the right with connecting lines. The Skip-gram layout follows a similar structure. Writing SVG diagrams... Writing the HTML structure... ```html
